hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: 15th percentile — a real, wide gap from the Top 100 median (1432 HPS vs 435 here). HPM (2.22) is also under half the Top 100 median (5.16), so this reads as a throughput and efficiency gap together, not just one metric. Overheal (72.7%) exceeds even the single worst Top 100 parse on this boss (58.4%), the first sign of a pattern that recurs across most of this raid (see the raid overview).
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lippies's Flash Heal casts this kill are guid 25233 (real mana cost 400), a genuinely different real rank from the Top 100 benchmark's dominant Flash Heal entry (guid 25235, real mana cost 470) — different guid, not a duplicate row. Real spell mix otherwise tracks the benchmark's shape closely (same top spells, similar order); the clearest gap is Power Word: Shield at 0% here vs 5.6% average.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Power Word: Shield's ~0% Top 100 usage on this boss is real but misleading (see the caveat above) — Lippies's own report cast it 8 times across the raid night, just not on this kill. Blessing of Life was cast once, self — a real overcast relative to the Top 100 sample's 6% typical usage here, though a low-stakes one. Zero real deaths this kill, so no cooldown/death correlation to check.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (34.8%) is close to the Top 100 average's top target (36.9%), and overall coverage (69.7% vs 70.6%) is within normal variance — targeting shape here matches the norm; the real gap on this kill is throughput and overheal, not target spread.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: 23rd percentile, the highest of the raid's first two kills. Overheal (44.7%) still exceeds the sample's single worst parse (40.5%) — active time (97.5%) is genuinely above the Top 100 average (95.4%) though, a real positive on this kill specifically.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The clearest gap on this kill: Circle of Healing is only 11.2% of Lippies's healing vs. 70.9% average — the Top 100 sample leans on it far more heavily than Lippies does, compensating with more Flash Heal (57.7% on Lippies's own guid 25233) and Prayer of Mending (23.8% vs. 8% average) than the norm. This same Circle of Healing gap recurs on most bosses this raid (see the raid overview).
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: 3 real deaths this kill (Captinspanky, Vajony, Hatchpeppers, all within 97s of each other). Lippies's Mana Potion (self) lands 7 seconds before Captinspanky's death — too close to be a response to it, and Mana Potion isn't a heal regardless. Vajony's and Hatchpeppers's deaths sit 13-37 seconds from Lippies's Shadowfiend/Chakra casts, not close enough to read as related. Power Word: Shield was never cast — Lippies's one real other-targeted defensive tool went unused across this death-heavy kill.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (47.8%) and top target share (12.9%) both track the Top 100 average closely (45.8%/11.9%) — targeting spread here is normal; the real gaps this kill are spell composition and HPM.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: 30th percentile is Lippies's best of the raid's first four kills, and active time (99.3%) genuinely beats the Top 100 average (96.2%). But this was also the raid's deadliest kill (8 real deaths against a 3954-HPS Top 1 parse), and overheal (47.4%) still exceeds the sample's worst (38.4%).
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The largest single spell-composition gap of the raid: Circle of Healing is only 2.7% of Lippies's healing here vs. 76.9% average — the Top 100 sample's dominant spell on this boss barely features in Lippies's own mix, replaced almost entirely by Flash Heal (58.3%, guid 25233) and Renew (19.3%, more than double the 8.1% norm).
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: This is the one kill this raid where Power Word: Shield sees real, active use — 4 real casts on 4 different raiders (Raezy, Churbert, Hatchpeppers, Banepain). The fight's first five deaths (Churbert, Hez, Captinspanky, Vinnyvoss, Destromental) all land 13-38 seconds before Lippies's Mana Potion — not correlated, since Mana Potion isn't a heal and the timing runs the wrong direction. The remaining three deaths (Destromental and Churbert dying a second time, plus Captinspanky's second death) sit 7-41 seconds after Lippies's Blessing of Life (self) — a real, checkable pattern of reactive self-preservation following a chaotic death cluster, not a claim that it prevented or caused any specific death.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (51.0%) runs about 11 points above the Top 100 average (40.2%) — a real but modest concentration gap, plausibly related to a chaotic, high-death fight where healing gets spread reactively rather than in a steady rotation.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: Lippies's best percentile of the raid so far (33rd), and overheal (27.3%) sits close to the Top 100 median for once, not exceeding the sample's worst — a real, genuine improvement on this kill. The one real weak spot is active time (88.7%), well below the 97.3% average, worth checking against this specific pull's own gameplay.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Same recurring pattern: Circle of Healing at 11.5% vs. 63.2% average — the third consecutive kill where the Top 100 sample's dominant heal is a small share of Lippies's own mix, replaced mostly by more Flash Heal spam (67.1% on Lippies's own guid vs 0% for the benchmark's own dominant Flash Heal guid).
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: A genuine standout: Fear Ward cast once, on Coday — the Top 100 sample shows 0% usage on this boss, real, uncommon usage against the norm. Shlicktree's and Deepbowlz's deaths land just 5-6 seconds after that Fear Ward cast — close enough to note, but Fear Ward grants fear immunity, not healing, so this isn't a missed-save timing; Danceswtrees's later death sits 29 seconds from the nearest cooldown (Mana Potion), too far to read as related.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (49.2%) and top target share (16.1% vs. 16.7%) both match the Top 100 norm almost exactly — targeting is not a factor on this kill.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: Active time (99.1%) is near the top of the Top 100 range, and this was a clean, deathless kill — but HPS (728) is still under half the median (1813), and overheal (55.2%) again exceeds the sample's single worst parse (45.4%).
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing again well below norm (15.8% vs. 58.3%) — the fourth consecutive kill with this gap. Prayer of Mending (20.2%) runs well above its own average (8.5%) here, suggesting it's substituting for some of the missing AoE-heal share rather than the deficit being pure throughput loss.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Power Word: Shield's single cast went to Turbarct, who is also Lippies's single largest healing target this kill (see section 04) — a coherent, tank-focused pattern rather than a random cast, and a real overcast relative to this boss's ~0% Top 100 usage (see the caveat above). Zero deaths this kill, so no cooldown/death timing to check.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: The largest targeting gap of the raid so far: 48.3% of Lippies's healing went to one target (Turbarct) vs. the Top 100 average's top target getting just 17% — nearly triple the normal concentration. This reads as real, dedicated tank-healing on a fight where Turbarct is clearly the primary damage sink, not a targeting mistake, but it is a genuinely different pattern from the rest of the sample.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: This is the longest kill of the raid (498s) and the first where overheal (50.6%) actually falls within the Top 100 sample's real range (best 15.4% to worst 74.6%), rather than exceeding the worst parse — a real, if modest, improvement over most of the raid. HPS (556) is still well under half the median (1294).
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: On this long fight the Top 100 sample spreads healing more evenly across Circle of Healing, Renew, and its own Flash Heal rank (29.7%/24.9%/23.2%); Lippies instead leans over 60% into Flash Heal (guid 25233) alone. A real, sustained-fight difference in playstyle, not just a spot deviation.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: 5 real deaths this kill (Purehentai, Destromental, Deepbowlz, Vinnyvoss all within 20s of each other, then Captinspanky roughly 4.5 minutes later). All four of the early cluster land 56-77 seconds after Lippies's one Mana Potion (self) — too far apart to connect, and Mana Potion isn't a heal regardless. Captinspanky's later death sits 28 seconds before a Chakra cast, also too far to read as related. Power Word: Shield went uncast across this entire 498-second fight — Lippies's one real other-targeted defensive cooldown had zero real usage over the raid's longest kill.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (44.7%) and top target share (13.6% vs. 13.2%) both track the Top 100 norm closely — targeting is not a factor on this kill.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: Lippies's lowest percentile of the raid so far (12th) — HPS (410) is under a third of the Top 100 median (1333), and overheal (67.1%) again exceeds the sample's single worst parse (46.7%) by just over 20 points, the largest such gap yet.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The largest Circle of Healing gap of the raid: 1.6% here vs. 42.9% average. On this kill Lippies leans into Renew instead (44% vs. 14.4% average, a 3x over-index) — Al'ar's real air/ground phase-swap mechanic involves repeated movement, where a HoT that doesn't require standing in range is a plausible, sensible substitute for a channeled or GCD-heavy AoE heal; stated here as a plausible read of the real numbers, not a confirmed cause.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: HPM (2.39) is the lowest efficiency ratio of the raid relative to its own median so far — well under half. Zero deaths this kill, so no cooldown/death timing to check; Power Word: Shield's single real cast went to Vajony, a real overcast relative to this boss's ~0% Top 100 usage norm.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (66.9%) runs well above the Top 100 average (49.4%) — a real, notable concentration gap, though top target share itself (21.2% vs. 18.6%) is close to normal, so this is spread across the top 5 broadly rather than one single over-focused target.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: Lippies's raw HPS (1585) is the highest of the raid in absolute terms, but this is also the raid's highest-demand fight (Top 100 median 3683 HPS) — 10th percentile, tied for the lowest of the raid. Overheal (47.2%) is roughly double the sample's single worst parse (23.5%), the largest such gap of the whole night, and this kill saw 6 real deaths, the raid's deadliest.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: A real, notable strategic difference rather than a deficiency: on this raid-wide-damage fight, Lippies leans heavily on Prayer of Healing (65.3%) where the Top 100 sample leans on Circle of Healing instead (81.4%) — both are AoE heals well-suited to this encounter's damage profile, just a different real spell choice, not a missing one.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: The only kill this raid without a food buff active at pull start, a real, specific gap against a 97% Top 100 norm. Six real deaths this kill: Banepain's and Raezy's land 17-20 seconds after Lippies's Shadowfiend cast (not a heal, so not a save); the simultaneous Churbert/Purehentai death and Sackajaweeba's death sit 7-34 seconds from Lippies's Chakra cast; Hez's death lands just 2 seconds before a Mana Potion. None of these represent a missed other-targeted save — Lippies's self-only cooldowns (Shadowfiend, Chakra, 2x Blessing of Life) all fired, but Power Word: Shield, the one tool that could have helped someone else, never did.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (67.9%) is nearly double the Top 100 average (37.7%) — a real, substantial spread-healing pattern, consistent with the Prayer of Healing/Circle of Healing-heavy AoE playstyle already seen in section 02 on this raid-wide-damage fight. Lippies's own name appears as the #2 real target, unsurprising on a fight with heavy raid damage.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: Tied for the raid's lowest percentile (10th). Overheal (71.5%) again exceeds the sample's worst parse (58.5%), and active time (93.0%) is a real few points below the median (96.3%) — a genuine gap on this specific kill, more pronounced than most others this raid.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The closest spell-composition match to the Top 100 norm of any kill this raid: Flash Heal + Circle of Healing dominate both Lippies's mix (45.7%/35.4%) and the benchmark's (42.9% on its own Flash Heal rank + 40.6% Circle of Healing), in similar proportions. The composition isn't the story on this kill — active time and overheal are.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Real, active use of both other-targeted cooldowns this kill: Power Word: Shield twice (Paladanwood, Destromental) and Fear Ward once (Paladanwood) — Fear Ward is rare in the Top 100 sample here too (only 8% used it), so this is one of the rarer, more proactive kills for utility usage even though HPM (2.31) is Lippies's lowest of the raid. Zero real deaths this kill.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (48.9% vs. 49.3%) essentially matches the Top 100 norm — targeting is a non-factor here, same as spell composition.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: Lippies's lowest percentile of the entire raid (7th), on the night's second-longest kill (476s). Active time (88.5%) is below even this fight's own lower sample average (91.3%) — Kael'thas's real forced-movement phases pull every parse's active time down some, but Lippies's still trails. Overheal (66.6%) again exceeds the sample's worst (52.3%).
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing again below norm (17% vs. 44.4%) — the seventh kill this raid with this pattern. Otherwise Lippies's mix (Renew, Prayer of Mending, Prayer of Healing all close to their own averages) tracks the benchmark reasonably well outside of the Flash Heal/Circle of Healing split already seen throughout the night.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Kael'thas is the one fight all raid where Fear Ward is genuinely expected — 87% of the Top 100 sample casts it here, a huge jump from every other boss (0-31% elsewhere), and Lippies matched that real norm with a cast on Paladanwood. Four real deaths this kill: Banepain's and Sackajaweeba's land 43 seconds either side of Lippies's Mana Potion casts, and the simultaneous Deepbowlz/Vinnyvoss death sits 63 seconds from the nearest one — none close enough to read as correlated, and Mana Potion isn't a heal regardless. Power Word: Shield went uncast again across this 476-second fight.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (43.0% vs. 42.5%) and top target share (14.2% vs. 13.3%) both essentially match the Top 100 norm — the second kill this raid (with Solarian) where targeting tracks the sample almost exactly.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: identical on 18 of 19 real equipment slots across all 10 real combatantinfo snapshots
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: The Feet slot (item 30100) and the Back slot (item 28765) both carry no permanent enchant across all 10 kills this raid night - two real, persistent gear gaps. Ring enchants aren't flagged, per this era's self-only Enchanting restriction. Every socketed slot carries a real gem (14 gem entries total across 6 sockets), confirmed identical on all 10 snapshots.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Overheal exceeds the Top 100 sample's single worst real parse on 8 of 10 kills (Hydross, Lurker, Leotheras, Morogrim, Alar, Void Reaver, Solarian, Kael'thas), and Circle of Healing usage falls well below the Top 100 average on 7 of 10 kills too (Lurker, Leotheras, Karathress, Morogrim, Alar, Void Reaver, Kael'thas - replaced mostly with more Flash Heal and Renew). These are the two most consistent, raid-wide gaps, and each shows up on the individual boss pages below.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: 7 of 10 kills land under the 20th percentile (Hydross at 15th, Morogrim, Vashj, Alar, Void Reaver, Solarian tied at 10th, and Kael'thas the lowest at 7th) - HPS is under half the Top 100 median on every single kill this raid, the most consistent finding of the night. Karathress (33rd) and Leotheras (30th) are the two real bright spots, with Lurker (23rd) a distant third - all three still well below 50th. All 10 percentiles are exact report+fight WCL rankings, not fuzzy-matched.

GearCheckItems.Description: Mainhand carries a permanent enchant and an active temporary weapon oil, on all 10 kills
Ranged weapon (wand) was upgraded partway through the raid, after Lady Vashj
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: A real, benign upgrade, not a gearing gap - same wand equipped for the first 6 kills (Hydross through Lady Vashj), a different, newer wand for the last 4 (Al'ar through Kael'thas Sunstrider). Confirmed from real combatantinfo snapshots on both sides of the swap, not assumed.

GearCheckItems.Description: The shirt slot is genuinely filled (not the usual empty cosmetic slot) - no stat impact either way
